capabilities.description: Checks supplied affected genomic model identifiers against Alliance curation records.
capabilities.example_query: Validate AGM WB:WBStrain00000001 for this phenotype subject
capabilities.example_result: Returns a resolved AGM record, provider context, and validation status for the subject field.
capabilities.name: AGM identifier lookup

capabilities.description: Uses a supplied label and organism context to find the intended affected genomic model when an identifier is missing.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve the affected genomic model label N2 for WormBase
capabilities.example_result: Returns matching AGM candidates or marks the subject unresolved when the label is ambiguous.
capabilities.name: Label and taxon matching

capabilities.description: Preserves the original field path, submitted value, and lookup evidence so curators can review why a model was accepted or rejected.
capabilities.example_query: Check whether this phenotype annotation subject can use the submitted AGM
capabilities.example_result: Returns a per-target validation result with matched_id, matched_label, status, and evidence notes.
capabilities.name: Subject-field validation evidence

data_sources.description: Alliance curation records for affected genomic models and provider-owned model identifiers.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Cannot infer an AGM from paper text; it validates values supplied by extraction output or domain-pack validation inputs.
Ambiguous model labels require provider or taxon context for reliable resolution.
Does not validate gene or allele subjects; use Subject Entity Validation Agent or the type-specific validation agent instead.
Cannot create missing model records or assign new provider identifiers.
Only reports lookup evidence available from Alliance curation data.
agm.summary: Validates affected genomic model (AGM) subjects by matching supplied identifiers or labels to Alliance model-organism records.

capabilities.description: Checks supplied affected genomic model identifiers against Alliance curation records.
capabilities.example_query: Validate AGM WB:WBStrain00000001 for this phenotype subject
capabilities.example_result: Returns a resolved AGM record, provider context, and validation status for the subject field.
capabilities.name: AGM identifier lookup

capabilities.description: Uses a supplied label and organism context to find the intended affected genomic model when an identifier is missing.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve the affected genomic model label N2 for WormBase
capabilities.example_result: Returns matching AGM candidates or marks the subject unresolved when the label is ambiguous.
capabilities.name: Label and taxon matching

capabilities.description: Preserves the original field path, submitted value, and lookup evidence so curators can review why a model was accepted or rejected.
capabilities.example_query: Check whether this phenotype annotation subject can use the submitted AGM
capabilities.example_result: Returns a per-target validation result with matched_id, matched_label, status, and evidence notes.
capabilities.name: Subject-field validation evidence

data_sources.description: Alliance curation records for affected genomic models and provider-owned model identifiers.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Cannot infer an AGM from paper text; it validates values supplied by extraction output or domain-pack validation inputs.
Ambiguous model labels require provider or taxon context for reliable resolution.
Does not validate gene or allele subjects; use Subject Entity Validation Agent or the type-specific validation agent instead.
Cannot create missing model records or assign new provider identifiers.
Only reports lookup evidence available from Alliance curation data.
agm_validation.summary: Validates affected genomic model (AGM) subjects by matching supplied identifiers or labels to Alliance model-organism records.

capabilities.description: Search for alleles by symbol — partial symbols, synonyms, and any capitalization all work.
capabilities.example_query: Find all Ulk1 alleles in mouse
capabilities.example_result: Returns all alleles matching 'Ulk1' (e.g., Ulk1<sup>tm1Thsn</sup>) with IDs, symbols, names, and species
capabilities.name: Allele search (partial match)

capabilities.description: Find an allele by its exact official symbol. You can paste it the way it appears in a paper (for example Ulk1<tm1Thsn>) and it is matched automatically to the official superscript form.
capabilities.example_query: Find the allele Ulk1<tm1Thsn>
capabilities.example_result: Returns the exact matching allele with full details including MGI:3689906
capabilities.name: Exact symbol lookup

capabilities.description: Retrieve allele details directly by Alliance CURIE
capabilities.example_query: Tell me about MGI:3689906
capabilities.example_result: Returns allele symbol, name, species, and status for the given ID
capabilities.name: Allele ID lookup

capabilities.description: Look up multiple alleles in a single request
capabilities.example_query: Look up these alleles: e1370, n765, tm1234
capabilities.example_result: Returns validation results for each allele, with not_found list for any missing
capabilities.name: Batch validation

data_sources.description: Comprehensive allele/variant data from all Model Organism Databases, including symbols, names, synonyms, obsolete status, and extinction status
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database (AGR)

limitations: Only validates against Alliance group data - newly published alleles may not be in the database yet
Searches alleles only - genes, diseases, and phenotypes are handled by separate agents
Allele symbols written with angle brackets are matched to their superscript form automatically — you don't need to reformat them.
Very broad searches return at most 500 matches (100 by default), so narrow your search if you don't see the allele you expect.
allele.summary: Validates allele/variant identifiers against the Alliance Curation Database by symbol, ID, or gene association.

capabilities.description: Scans the whole paper for allele symbols, variant notations (including HGVS, the standard way of writing sequence variants), and genotype descriptions.
capabilities.example_query: Extract all alleles from this paper
capabilities.example_result: Returns allele candidates with exact notation as written, associated gene, and organism context
capabilities.name: Allele/variant candidate harvesting

capabilities.description: Distinguishes alleles from strains, transgene constructs, balancer chromosomes, and chromosomal deficiencies using organism-specific nomenclature rules
capabilities.example_query: Extract alleles from this C. elegans genetics paper
capabilities.example_result: Correctly classifies daf-2(e1370) as an allele while excluding N2 (strain) and muEx225 (transgene array)
capabilities.name: Entity classification

capabilities.description: Parses composite genotype strings into individual alleles (e.g., 'daf-2(e1370); daf-16(mu86)' becomes two separate entries)
capabilities.example_query: Parse the genotypes in this paper's strain table
capabilities.example_result: Returns individual alleles with gene associations and allele type classification
capabilities.name: Genotype string parsing

capabilities.description: Passes each proposed allele — with its exact notation, its likely gene, the organism, and the supporting quote — to the Allele Validation Agent, the stronger specialist that authoritatively confirms each one against the Alliance database. The extractor proposes; the validator confirms and settles the official allele ID.
capabilities.example_query: Extract the alleles and pass them on for confirmation
capabilities.example_result: Returns each proposed allele with its exact notation and supporting quote, ready for the Allele Validation Agent to confirm
capabilities.name: Hands alleles to the validator

data_sources.description: Hybrid semantic/keyword plus lexical-heavy search modes over uploaded PDF documents
data_sources.name: PDF Document Search (Weaviate)

data_sources.description: The specialist validator that takes the proposed alleles and authoritatively confirms each one against the Alliance database after extraction, filling in the official allele ID, symbol, and species when it finds a match.
data_sources.name: Allele Validation Agent

limitations: Requires an uploaded PDF document — cannot extract alleles from chat text alone

Transgene handling follows organism-specific rules: MGI retains stable inherited Cre-driver, reporter, and other transgenic lines as alleles, while transient expression and unlinked construct/reagent mentions are excluded
Balancer chromosomes and chromosomal deficiencies are classified as tools, not alleles
Confirming each allele against the Alliance database is the Allele Validation Agent's job; any unconfirmed or conflicting alleles stay visible to you as validator findings.
allele_extractor.summary: Extracts experimentally supported allele and variant mentions from uploaded PDFs, distinguishing alleles from strains, transgenes, and balancers.

capabilities.description: Search for alleles by symbol — partial symbols, synonyms, and any capitalization all work.
capabilities.example_query: Find all Ulk1 alleles in mouse
capabilities.example_result: Returns all alleles matching 'Ulk1' (e.g., Ulk1<sup>tm1Thsn</sup>) with IDs, symbols, names, and species
capabilities.name: Allele search (partial match)

capabilities.description: Find an allele by its exact official symbol. You can paste it the way it appears in a paper (for example Ulk1<tm1Thsn>) and it is matched automatically to the official superscript form.
capabilities.example_query: Find the allele Ulk1<tm1Thsn>
capabilities.example_result: Returns the exact matching allele with full details including MGI:3689906
capabilities.name: Exact symbol lookup

capabilities.description: Retrieve allele details directly by Alliance CURIE
capabilities.example_query: Tell me about MGI:3689906
capabilities.example_result: Returns allele symbol, name, species, and status for the given ID
capabilities.name: Allele ID lookup

capabilities.description: Look up multiple alleles in a single request
capabilities.example_query: Look up these alleles: e1370, n765, tm1234
capabilities.example_result: Returns validation results for each allele, with not_found list for any missing
capabilities.name: Batch validation

data_sources.description: Comprehensive allele/variant data from all Model Organism Databases, including symbols, names, synonyms, obsolete status, and extinction status
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database (AGR)

limitations: Only validates against Alliance group data - newly published alleles may not be in the database yet
Searches alleles only - genes, diseases, and phenotypes are handled by separate agents
Allele symbols written with angle brackets are matched to their superscript form automatically — you don't need to reformat them.
Very broad searches return at most 500 matches (100 by default), so narrow your search if you don't see the allele you expect.
allele_validation.summary: Validates allele/variant identifiers against the Alliance Curation Database by symbol, ID, or gene association.

capabilities.description: Presents structured extraction output in readable chat-first format
capabilities.example_query: Show me the extracted entities in chat
capabilities.example_result: Returns a readable, sectioned summary directly in chat
capabilities.name: Inline result rendering

limitations: Output is optimized for interactive reading, not file export
chat_output.summary: Formats and displays final results directly in the chat interface for immediate curator review.

capabilities.description: Presents structured extraction output in readable chat-first format
capabilities.example_query: Show me the extracted entities in chat
capabilities.example_result: Returns a readable, sectioned summary directly in chat
capabilities.name: Inline result rendering

limitations: Output is optimized for interactive reading, not file export
chat_output_formatter.summary: Formats and displays final results directly in the chat interface for immediate curator review.

capabilities.description: Search for a chemical by name and get its ChEBI ID. Handles partial names and synonyms, so you don't need the exact official spelling.
capabilities.example_query: Look up the ChEBI ID for glucose
capabilities.example_result: CHEBI:17234 (D-glucose) with formula C6H12O6
capabilities.name: Chemical Name to ChEBI ID Lookup

capabilities.description: Get the full details for a compound: its definition, molecular formula, standard chemical-structure codes (InChI and SMILES), and synonyms.
capabilities.example_query: Get details for CHEBI:17234
capabilities.example_result: D-glucose - An aldohexose used as a source of energy and metabolic intermediate. Formula: C6H12O6
capabilities.name: Compound Detail Retrieval

capabilities.description: Show the broader categories a compound belongs to, and the more specific compounds underneath it, within ChEBI.
capabilities.example_query: What are the parent classifications of glucose?
capabilities.example_result: is_a aldohexose (CHEBI:33917), is_a hexose (CHEBI:18133), is_a monosaccharide (CHEBI:35381)
capabilities.name: Ontology Classification Navigation

capabilities.description: Look up multiple chemicals in a single request. Returns results for each found compound and lists any terms not found.
capabilities.example_query: Look up these chemicals: glucose, ATP, ethanol
capabilities.example_result: Found: CHEBI:17234 (D-glucose), CHEBI:15422 (ATP), CHEBI:16236 (ethanol)
capabilities.name: Batch Chemical Lookup

data_sources.description: EBI-hosted curated ontology of molecular entities focused on small chemical compounds involved in biological processes, including drugs, metabolites, cofactors, and toxins.
data_sources.name: ChEBI (Chemical Entities of Biological Interest)

limitations: Cannot provide chemical-gene interactions (requires gene specialist agent)
Cannot provide drug targets or mechanisms of action
Cannot provide pathway information (pathways not in ChEBI)
Multiple results for common names (e.g., 'glucose' matches many stereoisomers) - selects most biologically relevant form
Every answer comes from a fresh, live lookup against ChEBI — it never answers from memory.
chemical.summary: Confirms chemical compound names against ChEBI (Chemical Entities of Biological Interest) and gives you the matching official ChEBI term and ID for a chemical annotation.

capabilities.description: Search for a chemical by name and get its ChEBI ID. Handles partial names and synonyms, so you don't need the exact official spelling.
capabilities.example_query: Look up the ChEBI ID for glucose
capabilities.example_result: CHEBI:17234 (D-glucose) with formula C6H12O6
capabilities.name: Chemical Name to ChEBI ID Lookup

capabilities.description: Get the full details for a compound: its definition, molecular formula, standard chemical-structure codes (InChI and SMILES), and synonyms.
capabilities.example_query: Get details for CHEBI:17234
capabilities.example_result: D-glucose - An aldohexose used as a source of energy and metabolic intermediate. Formula: C6H12O6

capabilities.description: Show the broader categories a compound belongs to, and the more specific compounds underneath it, within ChEBI.
capabilities.example_query: What are the parent classifications of glucose?
capabilities.example_result: is_a aldohexose (CHEBI:33917), is_a hexose (CHEBI:18133), is_a monosaccharide (CHEBI:35381)
capabilities.name: Ontology Classification Navigation

capabilities.description: Look up multiple chemicals in a single request. Returns results for each found compound and lists any terms not found.
capabilities.example_query: Look up these chemicals: glucose, ATP, ethanol
capabilities.example_result: Found: CHEBI:17234 (D-glucose), CHEBI:15422 (ATP), CHEBI:16236 (ethanol)
capabilities.name: Batch Chemical Lookup

data_sources.description: EBI-hosted curated ontology of molecular entities focused on small chemical compounds involved in biological processes, including drugs, metabolites, cofactors, and toxins.
data_sources.name: ChEBI (Chemical Entities of Biological Interest)

limitations: Cannot provide chemical-gene interactions (requires gene specialist agent)
Cannot provide drug targets or mechanisms of action
Cannot provide pathway information (pathways not in ChEBI)
Multiple results for common names (e.g., 'glucose' matches many stereoisomers) - selects most biologically relevant form
Every answer comes from a fresh, live lookup against ChEBI — it never answers from memory.
chemical_validation.summary: Confirms chemical compound names against ChEBI (Chemical Entities of Biological Interest) and gives you the matching official ChEBI term and ID for a chemical annotation.

capabilities.description: Checks a submitted term name against a specific Alliance controlled vocabulary.
capabilities.example_query: Validate relation term 'is_model_of' in the disease relation vocabulary
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matching vocabulary term, abbreviation, and validation status.
capabilities.name: Vocabulary term lookup

capabilities.description: Resolves vocabulary terms from abbreviations or synonyms when the canonical term name was not supplied.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve abbreviation 'IDA' in the evidence code vocabulary
capabilities.example_result: Returns the canonical vocabulary term or ambiguous candidates for curator review.
capabilities.name: Abbreviation and synonym matching

capabilities.description: Flags obsolete controlled vocabulary terms according to the field's accepted obsolete-term policy.
capabilities.example_query: Check whether this expression relation vocabulary term is still current
capabilities.example_result: Returns an accepted, obsolete, ambiguous, or unresolved result with evidence details.
capabilities.name: Obsolete-term checks

data_sources.description: Alliance controlled vocabulary rows, including names, abbreviations, synonyms, and obsolete status.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Requires the target vocabulary name or field context to avoid cross-vocabulary ambiguity.
Does not validate ontology identities; use Ontology Term Resolver Agent for ontology CURIEs or typed ontology labels.
Cannot decide biological correctness of a term choice outside the submitted field context.
Cannot create or update controlled vocabulary rows.
Common abbreviations may produce ambiguous results unless the vocabulary is specified.
controlled_vocabulary.summary: Validates controlled vocabulary terms by vocabulary name, term name, abbreviation, synonym, and obsolete state.

capabilities.description: Checks a submitted term name against a specific Alliance controlled vocabulary.
capabilities.example_query: Validate relation term 'is_model_of' in the disease relation vocabulary
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matching vocabulary term, abbreviation, and validation status.
capabilities.name: Vocabulary term lookup

capabilities.description: Resolves vocabulary terms from abbreviations or synonyms when the canonical term name was not supplied.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve abbreviation 'IDA' in the evidence code vocabulary
capabilities.example_result: Returns the canonical vocabulary term or ambiguous candidates for curator review.
capabilities.name: Abbreviation and synonym matching

capabilities.description: Flags obsolete controlled vocabulary terms according to the field's accepted obsolete-term policy.
capabilities.example_query: Check whether this expression relation vocabulary term is still current
capabilities.example_result: Returns an accepted, obsolete, ambiguous, or unresolved result with evidence details.
capabilities.name: Obsolete-term checks

data_sources.description: Alliance controlled vocabulary rows, including names, abbreviations, synonyms, and obsolete status.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Requires the target vocabulary name or field context to avoid cross-vocabulary ambiguity.
Does not validate ontology identities; use Ontology Term Resolver Agent for ontology CURIEs or typed ontology labels.
Cannot decide biological correctness of a term choice outside the submitted field context.
Cannot create or update controlled vocabulary rows.
Common abbreviations may produce ambiguous results unless the vocabulary is specified.
controlled_vocabulary_validation.summary: Validates controlled vocabulary terms by vocabulary name, term name, abbreviation, synonym, and obsolete state.

capabilities.description: Converts normalized extraction output into comma-separated tabular rows
capabilities.example_query: Export extracted genes and evidence as CSV
capabilities.example_result: Returns a downloadable CSV with one record per row
capabilities.name: CSV export

limitations: Nested structures may be flattened for tabular compatibility
csv_formatter.summary: Formats structured extraction results as a downloadable CSV file for spreadsheet workflows.

capabilities.description: Turns prior extraction output into one or more curator-owned review sessions.
capabilities.example_query: Hand these extracted findings to the curation workspace
capabilities.example_result: Creates review sessions in the curation inventory for the relevant adapters
capabilities.name: Review session creation

capabilities.description: Works as a terminal step for flows and batches after upstream agents have gathered evidence.
capabilities.example_query: Finish this batch by preparing curation review sessions
capabilities.example_result: Marks the handoff ready once review sessions have been materialized
capabilities.name: Batch and flow handoff

capabilities.description: Carries extraction candidates and supporting evidence into the curation interface without approving or submitting them.
capabilities.example_query: Prepare the extracted candidates for curator review
capabilities.example_result: Leaves candidates ready for curator decisions in the review workspace
capabilities.name: Evidence-preserving transition

limitations: Requires upstream extraction or curation prep output; it does not discover new evidence on its own
Does not replace curator review, approval, or final submission
Runs as a flow or batch terminal step rather than a standalone chat specialist
curation_handoff.summary: Creates curator review sessions from completed flow or batch extraction results.

capabilities.description: Pulls together what the earlier agents extracted, what you confirmed was in scope, and the supporting quotes, into one review package.
capabilities.example_query: Prepare the confirmed extraction findings for curator review
capabilities.example_result: Creates a curation review session seeded from upstream extraction output instead of re-reading the paper from scratch
capabilities.name: Upstream extraction intake

capabilities.description: Keeps the supporting quotes attached to each item, so the later automated steps work from exactly the evidence the extractor found.
capabilities.example_query: Create curation prep candidates with the supporting quotes attached
capabilities.example_result: Returns the items for review, each still linked to its supporting quote and any notes about uncertainty.
capabilities.name: Evidence-backed review session prep

capabilities.description: Checks that each item is complete and well-formed, and flags anything incomplete or uncertain before you open the review session.
capabilities.example_query: Validate the candidate structure before opening the curation workspace
capabilities.example_result: Returns only the in-scope items, plus warnings where a field still needs your attention.
capabilities.name: Structural validation before handoff

capabilities.description: Works best as the last step after extraction/validation agents have already gathered the evidence that should enter curation review
capabilities.example_query: Use this as the final step after gene extraction and validation
capabilities.example_result: Hands the curator a review-ready session rather than acting like a standalone extractor or summarizer
capabilities.name: Flow-final curation handoff

limitations: Requires upstream extraction output and evidence records; it does not discover new evidence on its own
Should not be used as a standalone extraction, PDF reading, or summarization step
Does not replace curator review or approval; it prepares the review session but does not submit final curation
The later automated steps still handle matching items to official records and saving them.
curation_prep.summary: Gathers everything the earlier agents found, checks that each item is complete and well-formed, and opens a ready-to-review session for you — keeping the supporting quotes attached to every item.

capabilities.description: Checks whether a submitted data provider abbreviation exists in Alliance curation data.
capabilities.example_query: Validate data provider WB
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matched provider, display name, and validation status.
capabilities.name: Provider abbreviation lookup

capabilities.description: Resolves provider display names or alternate names to their official Alliance abbreviation when possible.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve WormBase as a data provider
capabilities.example_result: Returns provider abbreviation WB with lookup evidence.
capabilities.name: Provider name matching

capabilities.description: Checks whether a provider matches the supplied NCBITaxon context for the curation payload.
capabilities.example_query: Check whether WB is valid for NCBITaxon:6239
capabilities.example_result: Returns a provider result with taxon_match status and mismatch notes when applicable.
capabilities.name: Taxon consistency check

data_sources.description: Alliance data provider and organism metadata used for provider abbreviations, display names, and taxon relationships.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Requires a provider abbreviation, provider name, or taxon context from upstream data.
Does not infer data provider ownership from paper affiliation or author text.
Cannot validate entity identifiers owned by the provider; use the relevant entity validation agent.
Cannot create new provider records or taxon relationships.
Provider-to-taxon checks are limited to relationships present in Alliance curation data.
data_provider.summary: Validates Alliance data provider abbreviations, display names, and provider-to-taxon consistency for submitted curation records.

capabilities.description: Checks whether a submitted data provider abbreviation exists in Alliance curation data.
capabilities.example_query: Validate data provider WB
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matched provider, display name, and validation status.
capabilities.name: Provider abbreviation lookup

capabilities.description: Resolves provider display names or alternate names to their official Alliance abbreviation when possible.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve WormBase as a data provider
capabilities.example_result: Returns provider abbreviation WB with lookup evidence.
capabilities.name: Provider name matching

capabilities.description: Checks whether a provider matches the supplied NCBITaxon context for the curation payload.
capabilities.example_query: Check whether WB is valid for NCBITaxon:6239
capabilities.example_result: Returns a provider result with taxon_match status and mismatch notes when applicable.
capabilities.name: Taxon consistency check

data_sources.description: Alliance data provider and organism metadata used for provider abbreviations, display names, and taxon relationships.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Requires a provider abbreviation, provider name, or taxon context from upstream data.
Does not infer data provider ownership from paper affiliation or author text.
Cannot validate entity identifiers owned by the provider; use the relevant entity validation agent.
Cannot create new provider records or taxon relationships.
Provider-to-taxon checks are limited to relationships present in Alliance curation data.
data_provider_validation.summary: Validates Alliance data provider abbreviations, display names, and provider-to-taxon consistency for submitted curation records.

capabilities.description: Find DOID identifiers for disease names using case-insensitive search
capabilities.example_query: Look up Alzheimer's disease
capabilities.example_result: Returns DOID:10652, name, definition, and synonyms
capabilities.name: Disease name lookup

capabilities.description: Retrieve disease information by DOID identifier
capabilities.example_query: What is DOID:14330?
capabilities.example_result: Returns Parkinson's disease with definition and relationships
capabilities.name: DOID lookup

capabilities.description: Find diseases by synonym names when the exact term is not found
capabilities.example_query: Look up Alzheimers dementia
capabilities.example_result: Finds DOID:10652 via synonym match
capabilities.name: Synonym search

data_sources.description: The Alliance's curated copy of the Disease Ontology — official disease names, their IDs, definitions, and synonyms.
data_sources.name: Alliance disease term records

limitations: Only queries Disease Ontology (DOID) terms - does not include other disease vocabularies
Does not provide gene-disease associations (use Gene Specialist for that)
Disease prevalence and statistics are not available from this validator path
Does not walk up or down the disease hierarchy (parent or child terms) and does not track a term's obsolete history — ask the Ontology Term Resolver for related terms.
Every answer comes from a live lookup against the Alliance's disease terms — it never answers from memory.
disease.summary: Confirms disease names against the Disease Ontology (DOID) and gives you the matching official disease term and its ID, so a disease annotation can be submitted with confidence.

capabilities.description: Scans the paper for disease names, condition descriptions, and disease model references across all sections
capabilities.example_query: Extract all disease mentions from this paper
capabilities.example_result: Returns disease candidates with the name as written, role classification, and experimental context
capabilities.name: Disease mention harvesting

capabilities.description: Categorizes each disease mention as primary subject, background context, comparative reference, or model context
capabilities.example_query: Extract diseases from this Alzheimer's model paper
capabilities.example_result: Classifies 'Alzheimer's disease' as primary and 'diabetes' as comparative, with supporting evidence
capabilities.name: Role classification

capabilities.description: Determines the relationship type between genes and diseases using Alliance annotation types: is_implicated_in, is_marker_for, contributes_to, is_model_of
capabilities.example_query: What gene-disease relationships are reported?
capabilities.example_result: Returns association types with evidence (e.g., LRRK2 is_implicated_in Parkinson's disease)
capabilities.name: Gene-disease association typing

capabilities.description: Passes each proposed disease — with the name as written, the gene-disease relationship, the data provider, the subject, and the supporting quote — to the Disease Validation Agent, the stronger specialist that authoritatively confirms each one. The extractor proposes; the validator looks the disease up in the Disease Ontology, confirms the vocabulary, and settles the result.
capabilities.example_query: Extract the diseases and pass them on for confirmation
capabilities.example_result: Returns each proposed disease, its relationship, provider, and subject, with the supporting quote, ready for the Disease Validation Agent to confirm
capabilities.name: Hands diseases to the validator

data_sources.description: Hybrid semantic/keyword plus lexical-heavy search modes over uploaded PDF documents
data_sources.name: PDF Document Search (Weaviate)

data_sources.description: The specialist validator that takes the proposed diseases and authoritatively confirms each one after extraction — matching the Disease Ontology term, the relationship vocabulary, and the data provider.
data_sources.name: Disease Validation Agent

limitations: Requires an uploaded PDF document — cannot extract diseases from chat text alone
Distinguishes diseases from phenotypes — observable traits in model organisms are phenotypes, not diseases
Diseases mentioned only in introduction/background without new experimental findings are excluded
Looking each disease up in the Disease Ontology and confirming the provider is the Disease Validation Agent's job; any unconfirmed or conflicting diseases stay visible to you as validator findings.
disease_extractor.summary: Extracts experimentally supported disease associations from uploaded PDFs, classifying disease roles and gene-disease relationship types for Alliance curation.

capabilities.description: Find DOID identifiers for disease names using case-insensitive search
capabilities.example_query: Look up Alzheimer's disease
capabilities.example_result: Returns DOID:10652, name, definition, and synonyms
capabilities.name: Disease name lookup

capabilities.description: Retrieve disease information by DOID identifier
capabilities.example_query: What is DOID:14330?
capabilities.example_result: Returns Parkinson's disease with definition and relationships
capabilities.name: DOID lookup

capabilities.description: Find diseases by synonym names when the exact term is not found
capabilities.example_query: Look up Alzheimers dementia
capabilities.example_result: Finds DOID:10652 via synonym match
capabilities.name: Synonym search

data_sources.description: The Alliance's curated copy of the Disease Ontology — official disease names, their IDs, definitions, and synonyms.
data_sources.name: Alliance disease term records

limitations: Only queries Disease Ontology (DOID) terms - does not include other disease vocabularies
Does not provide gene-disease associations (use Gene Specialist for that)
Disease prevalence and statistics are not available from this validator path
Does not walk up or down the disease hierarchy (parent or child terms) and does not track a term's obsolete history — ask the Ontology Term Resolver for related terms.
Every answer comes from a live lookup against the Alliance's disease terms — it never answers from memory.
disease_validation.summary: Confirms disease names against the Disease Ontology (DOID) and gives you the matching official disease term and its ID, so a disease annotation can be submitted with confidence.

capabilities.description: Reviews each experimental-condition component, including condition class, condition ID, chemical, taxon, relation, and free-text fields.
capabilities.example_query: Validate this experimental condition for a chemical treatment with sirolimus
capabilities.example_result: Returns component-level validation statuses and an overall condition decision.
capabilities.name: Condition component validation

capabilities.description: Uses curation-DB ontology-term lookups for ChEBI chemicals (CHEBITerm) and typed ontology condition terms when those fields are present.
capabilities.example_query: Check whether this condition ID and chemical term match the submitted condition
capabilities.example_result: Returns matched ontology or chemical identifiers plus unresolved or ambiguous component notes.
capabilities.name: Chemical and ontology checks

capabilities.description: Combines component evidence into one condition-level result so curators can see which fields block acceptance.
capabilities.example_query: Summarize whether this experimental condition is ready for curator review
capabilities.example_result: Returns accepted, needs_review, or unresolved status with field paths and evidence messages.
capabilities.name: Auditable condition summary

data_sources.description: Alliance curation records for condition relation vocabulary, taxa, data providers, and ontology-backed condition fields.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Validates submitted condition payloads; it does not extract experimental conditions from PDFs.
Component-level ambiguity can make the overall condition require curator review.
Does not decide whether an experiment is biologically well designed or statistically sufficient.
Cannot create missing ontology, chemical, vocabulary, taxon, or provider records.
Quantity and unit validation depends on the submitted field context and available vocabulary evidence.
experimental_condition.summary: Validates composite Alliance experimental-condition payloads by checking ontology, chemical, taxon, vocabulary, quantity, and provider components together.

capabilities.description: Reviews each experimental-condition component, including condition class, condition ID, chemical, taxon, relation, and free-text fields.
capabilities.example_query: Validate this experimental condition for a chemical treatment with sirolimus
capabilities.example_result: Returns component-level validation statuses and an overall condition decision.
capabilities.name: Condition component validation

capabilities.description: Uses curation-DB ontology-term lookups for ChEBI chemicals (CHEBITerm) and typed ontology condition terms when those fields are present.
capabilities.example_query: Check whether this condition ID and chemical term match the submitted condition
capabilities.example_result: Returns matched ontology or chemical identifiers plus unresolved or ambiguous component notes.
capabilities.name: Chemical and ontology checks

capabilities.description: Combines component evidence into one condition-level result so curators can see which fields block acceptance.
capabilities.example_query: Summarize whether this experimental condition is ready for curator review
capabilities.example_result: Returns accepted, needs_review, or unresolved status with field paths and evidence messages.
capabilities.name: Auditable condition summary

data_sources.description: Alliance curation records for condition relation vocabulary, taxa, data providers, and ontology-backed condition fields.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Validates submitted condition payloads; it does not extract experimental conditions from PDFs.
Component-level ambiguity can make the overall condition require curator review.
Does not decide whether an experiment is biologically well designed or statistically sufficient.
Cannot create missing ontology, chemical, vocabulary, taxon, or provider records.
Quantity and unit validation depends on the submitted field context and available vocabulary evidence.
experimental_condition_validation.summary: Validates composite Alliance experimental-condition payloads by checking ontology, chemical, taxon, vocabulary, quantity, and provider components together.

capabilities.description: Find genes by symbol, name, ID, or cross-reference
capabilities.example_query: Look up the gene daf-16
capabilities.example_result: Returns gene ID, symbol, name, species, and synonyms
capabilities.name: Gene lookup

capabilities.description: Validate multiple genes at once
capabilities.example_query: Look up these genes: daf-16, lin-3, unc-54, act-1
capabilities.example_result: Returns validation results for each gene
capabilities.name: Batch validation

data_sources.description: Comprehensive gene data from all MODs
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Only validates against Alliance group data
Some newly published genes may not be in the database yet
gene.summary: Validates gene identifiers against the Alliance Curation Database.

capabilities.description: Identifies and extracts gene expression patterns from PDFs including anatomical locations (tissues, cell types, organs), developmental stages, temporal qualifiers, and sex-specific expression.
capabilities.example_query: Extract all gene expression patterns from this paper
capabilities.example_result: Returns structured annotations with gene symbol, anatomy label, life stage label, GO cellular component, and evidence text with page numbers
capabilities.name: Expression Pattern Extraction

capabilities.description: Extracts detailed reagent information used for expression detection including reporter fusions, CRISPR knock-ins, antibodies, in situ probes, and transgenic constructs.
capabilities.example_query: What reagents were used to detect gene expression?
capabilities.example_result: Returns reagent details like type ('CRISPR_knockin'), name ('dmd-3::YFP'), genotype, and strain
capabilities.name: Reagent Information Capture

capabilities.description: Identifies and extracts negative expression evidence where genes are NOT expressed in specific locations or stages.
capabilities.example_query: Find tissues where the gene is not expressed
capabilities.example_result: Flags these as negative results — for example 'not detected in neurons'.
capabilities.name: Negative Evidence Capture

capabilities.description: Passes each expression observation — the gene, the tissue or cell type, the developmental stage, the data provider, and the reagent used — to the specialist validators, each backed by its supporting quote. The extractor proposes; the validators authoritatively confirm the official terms and the data provider.
capabilities.example_query: Extract the expression observations and pass them on for confirmation
capabilities.example_result: Returns each expression observation with its supporting quote, ready for the validators to confirm the official terms
capabilities.name: Hands expression details to the validators

data_sources.description: Hybrid semantic/keyword plus lexical-heavy search modes over uploaded PDF documents. Supports searching by query terms, filtering by section, and reading full sections or subsections.
data_sources.name: PDF Document Search (Weaviate)

data_sources.description: Specialist validators confirm the expression-relation vocabulary and the data provider after extraction. Confirming the gene, anatomy term, developmental stage, and reference against the database is still in development.
data_sources.name: Expression validators

limitations: Returns plain text output only - JSON conversion is handled by a separate Formatter Agent
Does NOT extract expression data from mutant phenotypes or experimental perturbations (heat shock, RNAi knockdown, drug treatment) - only baseline/wild-type expression
Does NOT annotate transgenic markers used solely for strain identification (e.g., rol-6, myo-2::GFP co-injection markers)
The extractor records the term wording it finds; turning that into an official ontology ID is the validators' or the Ontology Term Resolver's job.
Requires at least one document search or read tool call before returning results
gene_expression.summary: Reads an uploaded paper and pulls out where and when each gene is expressed — the tissues, cell types, developmental stages, and any sex-specific patterns — with the supporting quote, ready for the specialist validators to confirm.

capabilities.description: Identifies and extracts gene expression patterns from PDFs including anatomical locations (tissues, cell types, organs), developmental stages, temporal qualifiers, and sex-specific expression.
capabilities.example_query: Extract all gene expression patterns from this paper
capabilities.example_result: Returns structured annotations with gene symbol, anatomy label, life stage label, GO cellular component, and evidence text with page numbers
capabilities.name: Expression Pattern Extraction

capabilities.description: Extracts detailed reagent information used for expression detection including reporter fusions, CRISPR knock-ins, antibodies, in situ probes, and transgenic constructs.
capabilities.example_query: What reagents were used to detect gene expression?
capabilities.example_result: Returns reagent details like type ('CRISPR_knockin'), name ('dmd-3::YFP'), genotype, and strain
capabilities.name: Reagent Information Capture

capabilities.description: Identifies and extracts negative expression evidence where genes are NOT expressed in specific locations or stages.
capabilities.example_query: Find tissues where the gene is not expressed
capabilities.example_result: Flags these as negative results — for example 'not detected in neurons'.
capabilities.name: Negative Evidence Capture

capabilities.description: Passes each expression observation — the gene, the tissue or cell type, the developmental stage, the data provider, and the reagent used — to the specialist validators, each backed by its supporting quote. The extractor proposes; the validators authoritatively confirm the official terms and the data provider.
capabilities.example_query: Extract the expression observations and pass them on for confirmation
capabilities.example_result: Returns each expression observation with its supporting quote, ready for the validators to confirm the official terms
capabilities.name: Hands expression details to the validators

data_sources.description: Hybrid semantic/keyword plus lexical-heavy search modes over uploaded PDF documents. Supports searching by query terms, filtering by section, and reading full sections or subsections.
data_sources.name: PDF Document Search (Weaviate)

data_sources.description: Specialist validators confirm the expression-relation vocabulary and the data provider after extraction. Confirming the gene, anatomy term, developmental stage, and reference against the database is still in development.
data_sources.name: Expression validators

limitations: Returns plain text output only - JSON conversion is handled by a separate Formatter Agent
Does NOT extract expression data from mutant phenotypes or experimental perturbations (heat shock, RNAi knockdown, drug treatment) - only baseline/wild-type expression
Does NOT annotate transgenic markers used solely for strain identification (e.g., rol-6, myo-2::GFP co-injection markers)
The extractor records the term wording it finds; turning that into an official ontology ID is the validators' or the Ontology Term Resolver's job.
Requires at least one document search or read tool call before returning results
gene_expression_extraction.summary: Reads an uploaded paper and pulls out where and when each gene is expressed — the tissues, cell types, developmental stages, and any sex-specific patterns — with the supporting quote, ready for the specialist validators to confirm.

capabilities.description: Scans the full paper for gene symbols, names, and synonyms across all sections including titles, abstracts, results, figure legends, and tables
capabilities.example_query: Extract all gene mentions from this paper
capabilities.example_result: Returns harvested candidates with exact mention text, species context, and supporting evidence
capabilities.name: Gene candidate harvesting

capabilities.description: Filters out common-word gene names (e.g., 'not', 'can', 'white'), gene families vs individual genes, and author/reagent names before validation
capabilities.example_query: Extract genes from this Drosophila paper
capabilities.example_result: Correctly excludes 'white' as background strain while retaining 'white' as a studied gene when experimentally supported
capabilities.name: Disambiguation and false-positive filtering

capabilities.description: Retains only genes with direct experimental support in the paper — genes cited from prior work or mentioned speculatively are excluded with reason codes
capabilities.example_query: Which genes have experimental data in this paper?
capabilities.example_result: Returns retained genes with evidence text and page numbers, plus explicit exclusions with reason codes
capabilities.name: Evidence-based validation

capabilities.description: Passes each proposed gene — with its exact wording from the paper, the species, and the supporting quote — to the Gene Validation Agent, the stronger specialist that authoritatively confirms each one against the Alliance database. The extractor proposes; the validator confirms and settles the official gene ID.
capabilities.example_query: Extract the genes and pass them on for confirmation
capabilities.example_result: Returns each proposed gene with its supporting quote, ready for the Gene Validation Agent to confirm
capabilities.name: Hands genes to the validator

data_sources.description: Hybrid semantic/keyword plus lexical-heavy search modes over uploaded PDF documents
data_sources.name: PDF Document Search (Weaviate)

data_sources.description: The specialist validator that takes the proposed genes and authoritatively confirms or rejects each one against the Alliance database after extraction.
data_sources.name: Gene Validation Agent

limitations: Requires an uploaded PDF document — cannot extract genes from chat text alone
Genes mentioned only in references without experimental data in this paper are excluded
When a gene could belong to more than one species, it uses clues in the text; cases it can't settle are flagged for curator review.
Confirming each gene against the Alliance database is the Gene Validation Agent's job; any unconfirmed or conflicting genes stay visible to you as validator findings.
gene_extractor.summary: Reads an uploaded paper and proposes the genes that have real experimental support in it, with the quote that backs each one — ready for the Gene Validation Agent to confirm against the Alliance database.

capabilities.description: Search for GO terms using name keywords or partial matches. Results are ranked by relevance with obsolete terms flagged.
capabilities.example_query: Find the GO term for 'DNA binding'
capabilities.example_result: GO:0003677 (DNA binding) - molecular_function
capabilities.name: Search GO terms by name or keyword

capabilities.description: Retrieve detailed information about a GO term including name, aspect, definition, and obsolete status.
capabilities.example_query: What is GO:0003677?
capabilities.example_result: GO:0003677 - DNA binding (molecular_function): Interacting selectively and non-covalently with DNA.
capabilities.name: Get GO term information by ID

capabilities.description: Shows the more specific terms directly underneath a GO term, and how each one relates to it (for example "is a kind of," "is part of," or "regulates").
capabilities.example_query: What are the children of GO:0003677 (DNA binding)?
capabilities.example_result: Children include: GO:0003690 (double-stranded DNA binding) - is_a, GO:0003697 (single-stranded DNA binding) - is_a
capabilities.name: Navigate GO term hierarchy (children)

capabilities.description: Get all ancestor terms (broader/more general) of a GO term, showing the path to root terms.
capabilities.example_query: What are the ancestors of GO:0003677?
capabilities.example_result: Ancestors include: GO:0003676 (nucleic acid binding), GO:0005488 (binding), GO:0003674 (molecular_function)
capabilities.name: Navigate GO term hierarchy (ancestors)

capabilities.description: Look up multiple GO terms in a single request for efficient batch processing.
capabilities.example_query: Look up these GO terms: apoptotic process, kinase activity, cell division
capabilities.example_result: Returns structured results for all requested terms with not_found list for any missing
capabilities.name: Batch GO term lookups

data_types: GO term synonyms and secondary IDs
data_sources.description: EMBL-EBI's QuickGO service providing programmatic access to the Gene Ontology database with term information, hierarchy, and relationships.
data_sources.name: QuickGO REST API

limitations: Cannot retrieve gene-to-GO annotations (use GO Annotations Agent instead)
Cannot determine which genes have a specific GO term (use GO Annotations Agent instead)
Does not perform GO enrichment analysis
Every answer comes from a fresh, live lookup against the Gene Ontology — it never answers from memory.
gene_ontology.summary: Looks up Gene Ontology (GO) terms — their definitions, broader and narrower terms, and how they relate — across the three GO categories: molecular function, biological process, and cellular component.

capabilities.description: Search for GO terms using name keywords or partial matches. Results are ranked by relevance with obsolete terms flagged.
capabilities.example_query: Find the GO term for 'DNA binding'
capabilities.example_result: GO:0003677 (DNA binding) - molecular_function
capabilities.name: Search GO terms by name or keyword

capabilities.description: Find genes by symbol, name, ID, or cross-reference
capabilities.example_query: Look up the gene daf-16
capabilities.example_result: Returns gene ID, symbol, name, species, and synonyms
capabilities.name: Gene lookup

capabilities.description: Validate multiple genes at once
capabilities.example_query: Look up these genes: daf-16, lin-3, unc-54, act-1
capabilities.example_result: Returns validation results for each gene
capabilities.name: Batch validation

data_sources.description: Comprehensive gene data from all MODs
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Only validates against Alliance group data
Some newly published genes may not be in the database yet
gene_validation.summary: Validates gene identifiers against the Alliance Curation Database.

capabilities.description: Retrieve all GO annotations for a specific gene, showing the functions, processes, and cellular locations associated with that gene
capabilities.example_query: What GO annotations exist for WB:WBGene00000898?
capabilities.example_result: Returns annotations like GO:0003677 (DNA binding) with evidence code IDA, assigned by WB
capabilities.name: Gene annotation lookup

capabilities.description: Distinguish between manually curated annotations (IDA, IMP, IPI) and automatic/electronic annotations (IEA, IBA)
capabilities.example_query: What manually curated annotations does daf-2 have?
capabilities.example_result: Returns only the manually curated annotations, plus a count of how many are manual versus automatic
capabilities.name: Evidence code analysis

capabilities.description: Retrieve GO annotations for multiple genes in a single request
capabilities.example_query: Get GO annotations for: WB:WBGene00000912, WB:WBGene00001234
capabilities.example_result: Returns annotations for each gene with evidence codes and sources
capabilities.name: Batch annotation retrieval

capabilities.description: Categorize annotations by GO aspect: Molecular Function (MF), Biological Process (BP), or Cellular Component (CC)
capabilities.example_query: What molecular functions does this gene have?
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matching annotations grouped by GO category, each with its GO ID, term name, and evidence
capabilities.name: GO aspect classification

data_sources.description: Official GO API at api.geneontology.org providing gene annotations with evidence codes
data_sources.name: Gene Ontology Consortium API

limitations: Gene IDs must be in Alliance format (e.g., WB:WBGene00000898, HGNC:11998)
Cannot search for GO terms by keyword - use Gene Ontology Lookup Agent for that
Cannot find genes by GO term (e.g., 'what genes have kinase activity')
Cannot explore GO term hierarchy or parent/child relationships
Heavily annotated genes may have 500+ IEA annotations - manual annotations are prioritized when summarizing
go_annotations.summary: Retrieves existing GO annotations for specific genes from the Gene Ontology Consortium API, including evidence codes, curation sources, and annotation counts.

capabilities.description: Retrieve all GO annotations for a specific gene, showing the functions, processes, and cellular locations associated with that gene
capabilities.example_query: What GO annotations exist for WB:WBGene00000898?
capabilities.example_result: Returns annotations like GO:0003677 (DNA binding) with evidence code IDA, assigned by WB
capabilities.name: Gene annotation lookup

capabilities.description: Distinguish between manually curated annotations (IDA, IMP, IPI) and automatic/electronic annotations (IEA, IBA)
capabilities.example_query: What manually curated annotations does daf-2 have?
capabilities.example_result: Returns only the manually curated annotations, plus a count of how many are manual versus automatic
capabilities.name: Evidence code analysis

capabilities.description: Retrieve GO annotations for multiple genes in a single request
capabilities.example_query: Get GO annotations for: WB:WBGene00000912, WB:WBGene00001234
capabilities.example_result: Returns annotations for each gene with evidence codes and sources
capabilities.name: Batch annotation retrieval

capabilities.description: Categorize annotations by GO aspect: Molecular Function (MF), Biological Process (BP), or Cellular Component (CC)
capabilities.example_query: What molecular functions does this gene have?
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matching annotations grouped by GO category, each with its GO ID, term name, and evidence
capabilities.name: GO aspect classification

data_sources.description: Official GO API at api.geneontology.org providing gene annotations with evidence codes
data_sources.name: Gene Ontology Consortium API

limitations: Gene IDs must be in Alliance format (e.g., WB:WBGene00000898, HGNC:11998)
Cannot search for GO terms by keyword - use Gene Ontology Lookup Agent for that
Cannot find genes by GO term (e.g., 'what genes have kinase activity')
Cannot explore GO term hierarchy or parent/child relationships
Heavily annotated genes may have 500+ IEA annotations - manual annotations are prioritized when summarizing
go_annotations_lookup.summary: Retrieves existing GO annotations for specific genes from the Gene Ontology Consortium API, including evidence codes, curation sources, and annotation counts.

capabilities.description: Preserves nested objects and arrays for downstream programmatic use
capabilities.example_query: Export extraction results as JSON
capabilities.example_result: Returns hierarchical JSON matching extracted structure
capabilities.name: Structured JSON export

limitations: File is optimized for machine consumption over spreadsheet readability
json_formatter.summary: Formats structured extraction results as downloadable JSON while preserving nested data.

capabilities.description: Validate exact ontology CURIEs against unique curation database ontology terms.
capabilities.example_query: Validate GO:0005634
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matched term facts or an unresolved result if the CURIE is absent.
capabilities.name: Exact CURIE lookup

capabilities.description: Resolve labels or synonyms using an explicit ontology_term_type such as DOTerm, ECOTerm, GOTerm, MPTerm, or WBPhenotypeTerm.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve 'abnormal locomotor behavior' as an MPTerm
capabilities.example_result: Returns one resolved term or preserves ambiguous candidates for curator review.
capabilities.name: Typed label and synonym lookup

capabilities.description: Resolve anatomy and developmental stage labels using provider context for species-specific term families.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve 'linker cell' for WormBase anatomy
capabilities.example_result: Returns the provider-appropriate ontology term or unresolved candidates.
capabilities.name: Provider-scoped anatomy and life-stage lookup

capabilities.description: Resolve Gene Ontology labels using an optional GO aspect filter.
capabilities.example_query: Resolve 'nucleus' as a GO cellular component
capabilities.example_result: Returns GO term candidates with lookup context.
capabilities.name: GO aspect lookup

data_sources.description: PostgreSQL database containing ontology terms and synonyms from Alliance curation data.
data_sources.name: AGR Curation Database

limitations: Cannot extract labels from PDFs; receives terms from prior extraction output or domain-pack validator inputs.
Cannot validate gene symbols; use Gene Validation Agent instead.
Cannot validate chemicals as ChEBI entities; use Chemical Validation Agent instead.
Cannot create new ontology terms; only resolves existing terms.
Requires ontology_term_type for generic label or synonym lookup.
Requires data_provider for provider-scoped anatomy and life-stage lookup.
ontology_term_validation.summary: Resolves exact ontology CURIEs and typed ontology-term labels with package lookup helpers while preserving ambiguous or unresolved candidates.

capabilities.description: Query orthologs for a given gene ID across all supported species (human, mouse, fly, worm, zebrafish, yeast, rat)
capabilities.example_query: Find orthologs for WB:WBGene00000898 (daf-16)
capabilities.example_result: Returns human FOXO3 (HGNC:3821) with high confidence, mouse Foxo3 (MGI:1890077) with high confidence, plus orthologs in other species
capabilities.name: Find gene orthologs

capabilities.description: Retrieve confidence levels (high, moderate, low) based on agreement between multiple prediction algorithms
capabilities.example_query: What is the confidence for the daf-16 to FOXO3 orthology?
capabilities.example_result: High confidence - 8 of 10 algorithms agree (Ensembl Compara, InParanoid, OMA, OrthoFinder, etc.)
capabilities.name: Get orthology confidence scores

capabilities.description: Find the single best-scoring ortholog in each species.
capabilities.example_query: What is the best human ortholog for daf-2?
capabilities.example_result: INSR (HGNC:6091) — flagged as the best match for human
capabilities.name: Identify best-scoring orthologs

capabilities.description: Show which orthology prediction algorithms support or do not support each relationship
capabilities.example_query: Which algorithms predict the daf-16/FOXO3 orthology?
capabilities.example_result: Matched: Ensembl Compara, InParanoid, OMA, PANTHER; Not matched: Xenbase, Hieranoid
capabilities.name: List prediction methods

data_sources.description: Public REST API providing DIOPT-aggregated orthology predictions from multiple algorithms
data_sources.name: Alliance of Genome Resources Orthology API

limitations: Gene ID must be in Alliance format with prefix (e.g., WB:WBGene00000898, not just WBGene00000898)
Needs a confirmed gene ID, not just a symbol — run the gene through the Gene Validation Agent first.
Does not find paralogs (within-species gene duplications)
Some genes have no orthologs in certain species (this is valid biological data, not an error)
orthologs.summary: Finds a gene's orthologs (the matching genes in other species) and tells you how confident the prediction is, based on how many ortholog-prediction methods agree (the DIOPT approach).

capabilities.description: Query orthologs for a given gene ID across all supported species (human, mouse, fly, worm, zebrafish, yeast, rat)
capabilities.example_query: Find orthologs for WB:WBGene00000898 (daf-16)
capabilities.example_result: Returns human FOXO3 (HGNC:3821) with high confidence, mouse Foxo3 (MGI:1890077) with high confidence, plus orthologs in other species
capabilities.name: Find gene orthologs

capabilities.description: Retrieve confidence levels (high, moderate, low) based on agreement between multiple prediction algorithms
capabilities.example_query: What is the confidence for the daf-16 to FOXO3 orthology?
capabilities.example_result: High confidence - 8 of 10 algorithms agree (Ensembl Compara, InParanoid, OMA, OrthoFinder, etc.)
capabilities.name: Get orthology confidence scores

capabilities.example_query: What is the best human ortholog for daf-2?
capabilities.example_result: INSR (HGNC:6091) — flagged as the best match for human
capabilities.name: Identify best-scoring orthologs

capabilities.description: Show which orthology prediction algorithms support or do not support each relationship
capabilities.example_query: Which algorithms predict the daf-16/FOXO3 orthology?
capabilities.example_result: Matched: Ensembl Compara, InParanoid, OMA, PANTHER; Not matched: Xenbase, Hieranoid
capabilities.name: List prediction methods

data_sources.description: Public REST API providing DIOPT-aggregated orthology predictions from multiple algorithms
data_sources.name: Alliance of Genome Resources Orthology API

limitations: Gene ID must be in Alliance format with prefix (e.g., WB:WBGene00000898, not just WBGene00000898)
Needs a confirmed gene ID, not just a symbol — run the gene through the Gene Validation Agent first.
Does not find paralogs (within-species gene duplications)

capabilities.description: Find specific content within the PDF using semantic, keyword, or exact-token lexical search
capabilities.example_query: Find all mentions of insulin signaling pathway
capabilities.example_result: Returns relevant passages with page numbers
capabilities.name: Full-text search

capabilities.description: Extract data tables and convert to structured format
capabilities.example_query: Extract the gene expression table from results section
capabilities.example_result: Returns structured data with headers and values
capabilities.name: Table extraction

capabilities.description: Read specific sections of the paper
capabilities.example_query: Read the Methods section
capabilities.example_result: Returns full text of the specified section
capabilities.name: Section navigation

data_sources.description: Scientific papers indexed in Weaviate
data_sources.name: PDF Documents

limitations: Document must be loaded in Weaviate before use
Very large tables may be truncated
Figure analysis is limited to captions only
pdf_extraction.summary: Extracts text, tables, and structured data from scientific papers using hybrid and lexical document search modes.

capabilities.description: Scans the paper for phenotype descriptions, observable traits, and mutant characterizations across results, figures, and tables
capabilities.example_query: Extract all phenotypes from this paper
capabilities.example_result: Returns phenotype candidates with the description as written, associated genotype, and experimental evidence
capabilities.name: Phenotype candidate harvesting

capabilities.description: Splits composite phenotype descriptions into individual assertions (e.g., 'small body size and reduced fertility' becomes two separate phenotypes)
capabilities.example_query: Extract phenotypes from this C. elegans genetics paper
capabilities.example_result: Returns individual phenotype assertions with polarity (gain/loss), severity qualifiers, and penetrance when stated
capabilities.name: Phenotype decomposition

capabilities.description: Associates each phenotype with the specific genotype, allele, or experimental condition that causes it, including conditional and negative results
capabilities.example_query: Which alleles cause which phenotypes?
capabilities.example_result: Returns phenotype-genotype pairs with experimental context (e.g., daf-2(e1370) → extended lifespan)
capabilities.name: Genotype-phenotype linking

capabilities.description: Passes each proposed phenotype — with the trait as written, the organism, the subject, and the supporting quote — to the phenotype term validator, the stronger specialist that authoritatively confirms each one. The extractor proposes; the validator confirms and settles the official ontology term.
capabilities.example_query: Extract the phenotypes and pass them on for confirmation
capabilities.example_result: Returns each proposed phenotype with its supporting quote, ready for the validator to confirm the official term
capabilities.name: Hands phenotypes to the validator

data_sources.description: Hybrid semantic/keyword plus lexical-heavy search modes over uploaded PDF documents
data_sources.name: PDF Document Search (Weaviate)

data_sources.description: The phenotype term validator confirms the official term for supported WormBase (WB) and Mouse Genome Informatics (MGI) phenotypes after extraction. Phenotypes from other databases aren't supported yet, so those are set aside rather than confirmed.
data_sources.name: Phenotype term validator

limitations: Requires an uploaded PDF document — cannot extract phenotypes from chat text alone
Distinguishes phenotypes from diseases — clinical conditions are handled by the Disease Extraction Agent
Wild-type/control observations are baselines, not phenotype assertions
The extractor proposes the trait wording only; confirming the official ontology term is the phenotype term validator's job after extraction.
phenotype_extractor.summary: Extracts experimentally supported phenotype assertions from uploaded PDFs, capturing genotype-phenotype links, polarity, severity, and supporting evidence.

capabilities.description: Looks up exact PMID, DOI, AGRKB, or MOD reference identifiers for a source paper.
capabilities.example_query: Validate reference PMID:12345678
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matched reference identifier, title, publication year, and validation status.
capabilities.name: Identifier lookup

capabilities.description: Searches by supplied title or citation text when an exact identifier is missing.
capabilities.example_query: Find the Alliance reference for title 'Regulation of daf-16 in longevity'
capabilities.example_result: Returns best matching references or ambiguous candidates for curator review.
capabilities.name: Title and citation search

capabilities.description: Preserves lookup evidence for source_reference fields used by downstream curation payloads.
capabilities.example_query: Validate the source reference for this allele association
capabilities.example_result: Returns a per-reference result with matched CURIE, identifier evidence, and unresolved notes.
capabilities.name: Source-reference materialization

data_sources.description: Alliance-backed literature reference lookup service for publication identifiers, titles, and citation metadata.
data_sources.name: Alliance Literature Reference API

limitations: Requires an identifier, title, or citation string from upstream extraction or domain-pack input.
Fuzzy title and citation searches can return multiple plausible matches.
Does not read or summarize paper content; use PDF Extraction Agent for document text.
Cannot create missing literature records or correct external reference metadata.
Lookup quality depends on the Alliance literature service and indexed reference metadata.
reference.summary: Resolves source-paper references by PMID, DOI, AGRKB CURIE, MOD reference ID, title, or citation text through Alliance literature lookup.

capabilities.description: Looks up exact PMID, DOI, AGRKB, or MOD reference identifiers for a source paper.
capabilities.example_query: Validate reference PMID:12345678
capabilities.example_result: Returns the matched reference identifier, title, publication year, and validation status.
capabilities.name: Identifier lookup

capabilities.description: Searches by supplied title or citation text when an exact identifier is missing.
capabilities.example_query: Find the Alliance reference for title 'Regulation of daf-16 in longevity'
capabilities.example_result: Returns best matching references or ambiguous candidates for curator review.
capabilities.name: Title and citation search

capabilities.description: Preserves lookup evidence for source_reference fields used by downstream curation payloads.
capabilities.example_query: Validate the source reference for this allele association
capabilities.example_result: Returns a per-reference result with matched CURIE, identifier evidence, and unresolved notes.
capabilities.name: Source-reference materialization

limitations: Requires an identifier, title, or citation string from upstream extraction or domain-pack input.
Fuzzy title and citation searches can return multiple plausible matches.

reference_validation.summary: Resolves source-paper references by PMID, DOI, AGRKB CURIE, MOD reference ID, title, or citation text through Alliance literature lookup.

capabilities.description: Uses the submitted subject_type to choose the correct validation path for gene, allele, or AGM subjects.
capabilities.example_query: Validate this phenotype subject with subject_type allele and ID WB:WBVar0001
capabilities.example_result: Returns the selected validator route and the type-specific validation result.
capabilities.name: Subject type routing

capabilities.description: Keeps the submitted identifier, label, taxon, and field path together while validating the subject entity.
capabilities.example_query: Check subject label daf-16 with gene subject type for WormBase
capabilities.example_result: Returns matched gene evidence or unresolved notes tied to the original subject field.
capabilities.name: Identifier and label preservation

capabilities.description: Reports one validation outcome per submitted subject so mixed subject batches remain auditable.
capabilities.example_query: Validate these phenotype and disease annotation subjects
capabilities.example_result: Returns accepted, ambiguous, or unresolved subject results with selected validator metadata.
capabilities.name: Per-subject review decisions

data_sources.description: Alliance entity records used by gene, allele, and affected genomic model validation routes.
data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Requires an explicit subject_type; it does not guess whether a value is a gene, allele, or AGM.
Does not validate references, ontology terms, controlled vocabulary terms, or experimental conditions.
Ambiguous or missing type-specific lookup results still require curator review.
Cannot create missing subject records or assign new entity identifiers.
Only routes among the supported subject validation paths exposed by the package.
subject_entity.summary: Routes biological annotation subjects by explicit subject type to gene, allele, or affected genomic model validation paths.

capabilities.description: Uses the submitted subject_type to choose the correct validation path for gene, allele, or AGM subjects.
capabilities.example_query: Validate this phenotype subject with subject_type allele and ID WB:WBVar0001
capabilities.example_result: Returns the selected validator route and the type-specific validation result.
capabilities.name: Subject type routing

capabilities.description: Keeps the submitted identifier, label, taxon, and field path together while validating the subject entity.
capabilities.example_query: Check subject label daf-16 with gene subject type for WormBase
capabilities.example_result: Returns matched gene evidence or unresolved notes tied to the original subject field.
capabilities.name: Identifier and label preservation

capabilities.description: Reports one validation outcome per submitted subject so mixed subject batches remain auditable.
capabilities.example_query: Validate these phenotype and disease annotation subjects
capabilities.example_result: Returns accepted, ambiguous, or unresolved subject results with selected validator metadata.
capabilities.name: Per-subject review decisions

data_sources.name: Alliance Curation Database

limitations: Requires an explicit subject_type; it does not guess whether a value is a gene, allele, or AGM.

subject_entity_validation.summary: Routes biological annotation subjects by explicit subject type to gene, allele, or affected genomic model validation paths.

capabilities.description: Automatically determines which specialist agent should handle your question
capabilities.example_query: Find the gene daf-2
capabilities.example_result: Hands the question to the Gene Validation Agent
capabilities.name: Query routing

capabilities.description: Coordinates complex queries that need multiple agents
capabilities.example_query: Find all genes mentioned in the paper and validate them
capabilities.example_result: Sends the paper to the PDF Specialist first, then passes the genes it finds to the Gene Validation Agent
capabilities.name: Multi-step coordination

limitations: Routing decisions are based on query keywords and context
Very ambiguous queries may be routed to a general agent first
Not available in Flow Builder - works automatically in chat
supervisor.summary: Routes curator queries to the appropriate specialist agent - works behind the scenes.

capabilities.description: Tell the AI what data to extract from the paper
capabilities.example_query: Extract all gene names and their expression patterns from this paper
capabilities.example_result: The flow begins processing your request through the configured agents
capabilities.name: Define extraction tasks

capabilities.description: Provide background information about what you're looking for
capabilities.example_query: This paper describes insulin signaling in C. elegans. Find all relevant genes.
capabilities.example_result: Context is passed to downstream agents to improve accuracy
capabilities.name: Set curation context

limitations: Instructions should be clear and specific for best results
Complex multi-part requests may need to be broken into separate flows
task_input.summary: The starting point for curation workflows - defines what task the AI should perform.

capabilities.description: Generates tab-delimited output for pipelines that expect TSV input
capabilities.example_query: Export curated rows as TSV
capabilities.example_result: Returns a downloadable TSV with stable tab-delimited columns
capabilities.name: TSV export

limitations: Like CSV, complex nested fields may be flattened
tsv_formatter.summary: Formats structured extraction results as a downloadable TSV file for tab-delimited pipelines.
